assertions.description: In mcu_stm32_1.sch, the 8MHz crystal Y2 load capacitors C79 and C80 carry value='20pF' but MPN=CL10C360JB8NNNC whose description reads 'CAP CER 36PF 50V C0G/NP0 0603'. The analyzer faithfully reports the schematic value (20pF) in the crystal_circuits effective load calculation (CL_eff = 13pF based on 20pF). The actual capacitors are 36pF (CL_eff = 21pF). This is a schematic data inconsistency (design note, not an analyzer bug) but the 20pF value is used in the crystal load calculation, making the reported CL_eff inaccurate relative to what will be built.
assertions.aspirational_reason: op eq not supported, describes analyzer limitation

assertions.description: The sheet has 3 SN65HVD234 CAN transceivers: U5 (nets CAN3+/CAN3-/CAN3_TX/CAN3_RX), U6 (nets CAN1+/CAN1-/CAN1_TX/CAN1_RX), and U7 (nets CAN2+/CAN2-/CAN2_TX/CAN2_RX). bus_analysis.can reports only one entry with net='CAN' and transceiver=U7. The net 'CAN' has zero pins in the nets dict — it is a phantom net. The real per-channel nets CAN1/CAN2/CAN3 are not represented. U5 and U6 are completely absent from bus_analysis.can. The differential_pairs section correctly finds all three CAN pairs.
